reasoning: The Lean `IsCatenary` definition captures "all maximal chains of primes from p to q have the same length n" via the existential-over-n followed by universal quantification over maximal chains (where maximality is correctly encoded by the sublist-containment condition: any chain l' with l.toList as a sublist must equal l, meaning l cannot be refined by inserting more primes). The `LTSeries` (strictly increasing) matches the informal strict chain p_0 ⊂ p_1 ⊂ ... ⊂ p_e, with `head`/`last` matching endpoints. While the informal definition explicitly includes the "integer bounding all finite chains" clause that the Lean omits, for the specific ring A in the theorem (Noetherian with ringKrullDim ≤ 3), all chains are automatically bounded by dim+1, so the two definitions coincide for A. The hypotheses `IsNoetherianRing A`, `IsDomain A`, `UniqueFactorizationMonoid A`, and `ringKrullDim A ≤ 3` precisely match "Noetherian UFD of dimension ≤ 3". Using `p ≤ q` vs `p ⊂ q` is harmless (the p=q case is trivial). The theorem is logically equivalent to the informal statement in this context.
